moments.label: Morning briefing
moments.title: Your day is already organized before the first click
moments.body: The operating system assembles a calm morning briefing with priorities, reply-ready drafts, meeting prep, and protected focus windows before work begins.
moments.suggestedCopilotPrompt: Summarize my day, highlight risks, and protect my best focus window.

moments.label: Protected focus mode
moments.title: Quiet mode protects the work that actually moves the day
moments.body: Noncritical interruptions stay out. The system preserves resume markers, keeps only the next three actions visible, and surfaces escalation only when it truly matters.
moments.suggestedCopilotPrompt: Keep me in flow, capture where I stop, and only allow critical interruptions.

moments.label: Triage interruption
moments.title: A critical thread breaks through with a prepared next action
moments.body: The interruption is not a raw notification. It arrives as a summarized decision card with rationale, confidence, and an editable draft that can be approved without context switching.
moments.suggestedCopilotPrompt: Show me only the interruption that cannot wait and prepare the fastest safe response.

moments.label: Meeting briefing pack
moments.title: Walk into the meeting with context already assembled
moments.body: The operating system lowers context-switch cost by building a meeting pack with decisions, blockers, related messages, and suggested talking points before the meeting begins.
moments.suggestedCopilotPrompt: Prepare me for the next meeting with open questions, decisions, and talking points.

moments.label: End-of-day review
moments.title: The day ends with decisions captured and tomorrow already shaped
moments.body: The operating system closes open loops, records what moved, defers the right items into tomorrow’s focus lane, and generates a concise narrative of progress and remaining risk.
moments.suggestedCopilotPrompt: Summarize what moved, what is still open, and what belongs in tomorrow’s focus block.

queue.title: Approve customer scope reply
queue.reason: Reply SLA is approaching and next week workshop scope is still unconfirmed.
queue.expectedOutcome: Unblocks workshop planning and protects customer confidence.

queue.title: Protect blueprint focus block
queue.reason: The most valuable morning work is product-definition writing, and noncritical traffic should not fragment it.
queue.expectedOutcome: Preserves deep work and keeps the session resumable.

morningBriefing.summary: The day is front-loaded around one customer decision, one protected focus block, and one meeting that needs prep.

focusLane.title: Finalize Intelligent Work Layer product blueprint
focusLane.summary: Open decisions, repository context, and the next three steps are already staged so you can resume without reconstructing the work.

resumeMarker.title: Resume marker
resumeMarker.detail: You last stopped after defining the five product pillars and still need to write the trust model section.

focusLane.nextSmallestStep: Write the one-paragraph product definition before expanding the blueprint sections.
focusLane.flowMemory: When you return later, the operating system will reopen this exact work packet with the same next-step state.

activities.text: Morning summary generated with focus window recommendation.

copilot.currentSuggestion: Summarize what moved today, preserve the final decisions, and carry the right items into tomorrow’s focus lane.
copilot.draftPrompt: Prepare a concise close-of-day summary and identify the highest-value starting point for tomorrow.

starterPrompts.label: Summarize what changed since this morning
Convert the current thread into a work packet
Prepare me for the next meeting
